result.subject: CT result handling
result.body: This result should remain available in the internal workflow history.

result.label: work
result.title: CT report
result.content:
EXAMINATION:  CT HEAD W/O CONTRAST Q111 CT HEAD

INDICATION:  ___ with PMH of aortic stenosis s/p AVR (___), prosthetic
aortic valve endocarditis s/p replacement with mechanical aortic valve c/b
complete heart block s/p ICD (___), CAD s/p 2V CABG (___), AF on
coumadin, HTN, HFpEF (EF > 55% ___, HTN, DM2, CKD, prostate CA s/p
radical prostatectomy (___), left calf hematoma c/b compartment syndrome
(___), GIB s/p gastric duodenal artery embolization (___) who presents
with weakness/falls, found to have acute CHF exacerbation and ___ with anuric
renal failure.// Cocnern for bleed, supratherapeutic INR, AMS

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images of the brain were obtained without
contrast.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 4.0 s, 16.0 cm; CTDIvol = 46.7 mGy (Head) DLP =
747.3 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Head) = 747 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Comparison includes CT head without contrast on ___..

FINDINGS: 

There is no evidence of acute large territorial infarction,hemorrhage,edema,
or mass effect.  The sulci, ventricles and cisterns are within expected limits
for the degree of mild senescent related global cerebral volume loss. 
Re-demonstrated, are mild hypodensities in the subcortical and periventricular
white matter likely consistent with macrovascular ischemic disease.

There is no evidence of fracture.  The visualized portion of the paranasal
sinuses, mastoid air cells, and middle ear cavities are clear.  The visualized
portion of the orbits are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:


1. There is no acute infarct, hemorrhage, edema or mass effect on noncontrast
head CT.
2. Re-demonstrated, are mild hypodensities in the subcortical and
periventricular white matter likely due to microvascular  ischemic disease
